title: German Gas Industry Warns Over Wavering Support for Hydrogen Ramp-Up
content: German gas and water association DVGW on Wednesday warned that a ramp-up in low-carbon hydrogen technology could fall behind as participants feel discouraged by regulatory and political indecision. A survey taken from across hydrogen stakeholders in Europe's biggest economy found that the desired transition from carbon- emitting natural gas to hydrogen from renewable electricity via electrolysis is feared to be slowing. Speakers at in an online press conference during DVGW's annual congress said they expected more determined action and less bureaucracy to dispel doubts about high costs and too little availability.
author: Analysts Account
